scenario_name: Johnson & Johnson Talcum Powder Cancer Lawsuits 2018
description: Reuters investigation revealed J&J knew for decades that asbestos lurked in its baby powder. Stock dropped $40B, tens of thousands of lawsuits filed. Trust in the iconic brand dropped sharply. Tests legacy brand trust destruction via concealment revelation.

key_events.description: St. Louis jury awards $4.69 billion to 22 women who claimed J&J baby powder caused ovarian cancer. Largest talc verdict to date.
Reuters publishes bombshell investigation showing J&J knew about asbestos in baby powder since 1971 but concealed findings from regulators and public.
J&J issues voluntary recall of 33,000 bottles of baby powder after FDA finds asbestos in samples. First public admission of contamination.
Number of lawsuits against J&J exceeds 19,000. Company proposes controversial 'Texas Two-Step' bankruptcy maneuver for talc liabilities.
J&J announces it will discontinue talc-based baby powder globally, switching to cornstarch formula. Widely seen as implicit admission.

calibration_notes: Tests trust destruction in a legacy 'trusted brand' via concealment revelation. J&J was consistently ranked among America's most trusted companies (top 5 in Harris Poll). Key dynamics: the betrayal amplifier (trusted baby product containing carcinogens), investigative journalism as catalyst, multi-decade concealment making the cover-up worse than the crime, legal strategy (bankruptcy maneuver) backfiring reputationally, product discontinuation as implicit guilt admission, and the special vulnerability of health/baby product brands to safety revelations.
